testcase.name: test_del_method_session_close
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4381086736'>
@mock.patch("qtrade.questrade.FuturesSession.request", side_effect=mocked_access_token_requests_get) def test_del_method_session_close(mock_get): """ "This function tests the successful session closing.""" with mock.patch.object(FuturesSession, "close") as mock_close: > qtrade = Questrade(access_code="hunter2") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_questrade.py:463: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ qtrade/questrade.py:56: in __init__ self._get_access_token(save_yaml=save_yaml) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <qtrade.questrade.Questrade object at 0x105222b50>, save_yaml = True yaml_path = 'access_token.yml'
def _get_access_token( self, save_yaml: bool = False, yaml_path: str = "access_token.yml" ) -> TokenDict: """Get access token.
This internal method gets the access token from the access code and optionally saves it in access_token.yaml.
Parameters ---------- save_yaml: bool, optional Boolean to indicate if the token payload will be saved in a yaml-file. Default False. yaml_path: str, optional Path of the yaml-file that will be saved. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten. Defaults to access_token.yml
Returns ------- dict Dict with the access token data. """ url = TOKEN_URL + str(self.access_code) log.info("Getting access token...") future = self.session.get(url) # Changed to submit > data = future.result() # Get the response from the Future ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'MockResponse' object has no attribute 'result'
qtrade/questrade.py:136: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_get_access_token
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4381086800'>
@mock.patch("qtrade.questrade.FuturesSession.request", side_effect=mocked_access_token_requests_get) def test_get_access_token(mock_get): """This function tests the get access token method.""" > qtrade = Questrade(access_code="hunter2") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_questrade.py:471: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ qtrade/questrade.py:56: in __init__ self._get_access_token(save_yaml=save_yaml) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <qtrade.questrade.Questrade object at 0x105221510>, save_yaml = True yaml_path = 'access_token.yml'
def _get_access_token( self, save_yaml: bool = False, yaml_path: str = "access_token.yml" ) -> TokenDict: """Get access token.
This internal method gets the access token from the access code and optionally saves it in access_token.yaml.
Parameters ---------- save_yaml: bool, optional Boolean to indicate if the token payload will be saved in a yaml-file. Default False. yaml_path: str, optional Path of the yaml-file that will be saved. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten. Defaults to access_token.yml
Returns ------- dict Dict with the access token data. """ url = TOKEN_URL + str(self.access_code) log.info("Getting access token...") future = self.session.get(url) # Changed to submit > data = future.result() # Get the response from the Future ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'MockResponse' object has no attribute 'result'
qtrade/questrade.py:136: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_refresh_token_yaml
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4382837712'>
@mock.patch("builtins.open", mock.mock_open(read_data=ACCESS_TOKEN_YAML)) @mock.patch("qtrade.questrade.FuturesSession.request", side_effect=mocked_access_token_requests_get) def test_refresh_token_yaml(mock_get): """This function tests the refresh token method by using the yaml.""" qtrade = Questrade(token_yaml="access_token.yml") > qtrade.refresh_access_token(from_yaml=True)
tests/test_questrade.py:507: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <qtrade.questrade.Questrade object at 0x1053cc510>, from_yaml = True yaml_path = 'access_token.yml'
def refresh_access_token( self, from_yaml: bool = False, yaml_path: str = "access_token.yml" ) -> TokenDict: """Refresh access token.
This method refreshes the access token. This only works if the overall access has not yet expired. By default it will look for the yaml-file, but it could also look for the internal state
Parameters ---------- from_yaml: bool, optional [False] This parameter controls if the refresh token is sourced from a yaml file or if the attribute `access_token` is used (default). If True, the yaml-file will be updated. yaml_path: str, optional Path of the yaml-file that will be updated. Defaults to access_token.yml
Returns ------- dict Dict with the access token data. """ if from_yaml: old_access_token = get_access_token_yaml(yaml_path) else: old_access_token = self.access_token
url = TOKEN_URL + str(old_access_token["refresh_token"]) log.info("Refreshing access token...") future = self.session.get(url) # Changed to submit > data = future.result() # Get the response from the Future ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'MockResponse' object has no attribute 'result'
qtrade/questrade.py:197: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_refresh_token_non_yaml
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4381146384'>
@mock.patch("builtins.open", mock.mock_open(read_data=ACCESS_TOKEN_YAML)) @mock.patch("qtrade.questrade.FuturesSession.request", side_effect=mocked_access_token_requests_get) def test_refresh_token_non_yaml(mock_get): """This function tests the refresh token method without yaml use.""" qtrade = Questrade(token_yaml="access_token.yml") > qtrade.refresh_access_token()
tests/test_questrade.py:519: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <qtrade.questrade.Questrade object at 0x10522fa50>, from_yaml = False yaml_path = 'access_token.yml'
def refresh_access_token( self, from_yaml: bool = False, yaml_path: str = "access_token.yml" ) -> TokenDict: """Refresh access token.
This method refreshes the access token. This only works if the overall access has not yet expired. By default it will look for the yaml-file, but it could also look for the internal state
Parameters ---------- from_yaml: bool, optional [False] This parameter controls if the refresh token is sourced from a yaml file or if the attribute `access_token` is used (default). If True, the yaml-file will be updated. yaml_path: str, optional Path of the yaml-file that will be updated. Defaults to access_token.yml
Returns ------- dict Dict with the access token data. """ if from_yaml: old_access_token = get_access_token_yaml(yaml_path) else: old_access_token = self.access_token
url = TOKEN_URL + str(old_access_token["refresh_token"]) log.info("Refreshing access token...") future = self.session.get(url) # Changed to submit > data = future.result() # Get the response from the Future ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'MockResponse' object has no attribute 'result'
qtrade/questrade.py:197: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_get_account_id
testcase.failure:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4382087504'>
@mock.patch("builtins.open", mock.mock_open(read_data=ACCESS_TOKEN_YAML)) @mock.patch.object(FuturesSession, "request", side_effect=mocked_acct_id_get) def test_get_account_id(mock_get): """This function tests the account ID function.""" qtrade = Questrade(token_yaml="access_token.yml") > acct_id = qtrade.get_account_id() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_questrade.py:531: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ qtrade/questrade.py:238: in get_account_id response: Dict[str, List[Dict[str, int]]] = self._send_message("get", "accounts") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <qtrade.questrade.Questrade object at 0x105317750>, method = 'get' endpoint = 'accounts', params = None, data = None, json = None
def _send_message( self, method: str, endpoint: str, params: Optional[Dict] = None, data: Optional[Dict] = None, json: Optional[Dict] = None, ) -> Dict[str, Any]: # pylint: disable=R0913 """Send an API request.
Parameters ---------- method: str HTTP method (get, post, delete, etc.) endpoint: str Endpoint (to be added to base URL) params: dict, optional HTTP request parameters data: dict, optional JSON-encoded string payload for POST json: dict, optional Dictionary payload for POST
Returns ------- dict/list: JSON response """ if self.access_token is not None: url = self.access_token["api_server"] + "/v1/" + endpoint else: log.error("Access token not set...") raise Exception("Access token not set...") future = self.session.request(method, url, params=params, data=data, json=json, timeout=30) # Changed to submit > resp = future.result() # Get the response from the Future ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'MockResponse' object has no attribute 'result'
qtrade/questrade.py:95: AttributeError
